query: How do we handle JWT auth?

setup.title: JWT convention
setup.content: JWT auth uses RS256 token pairs and refresh tokens.

setup.title: Webhook policy
setup.content: Stripe webhooks require signature verification and idempotency.

setup.title: Rate limiting
setup.content: Rate limiting uses Redis and a sliding window.

setup.title: Observability
setup.content: Ship structured logs to Loki and retain thirty days.

setup.title: Production deploy
setup.content: Production runs on an Infomaniak VPS behind Traefik.

setup.title: Package publish
setup.content: Publish the CLI package to PyPI after contract validation.

query: How are automatic memory writes handled?

setup.title: Memory review policy
setup.content: Automatic memory writes land in a review queue before persistence.

setup.title: Project import
setup.content: Import markdown docs into the standalone memory engine.

query: What is the open source wedge?

setup.title: Open source wedge
setup.content: The wedge is durable memory for coding agents with explicit lifecycle and contradictions.

setup.title: Transcript search
setup.content: Transcript search is useful but not enough for durable memory.
